quick_facts.label: What this node covers
quick_facts.value: MBA in Agri-Business Management is a specialised, management-focused postgraduate route for food, agriculture, rural and allied-sector value chains. The title is not fully standardised across institutions: for example, IIM Ahmedabad awards an MBA in Food and Agri-business Management, while MANAGE offers an AICTE-recognised PGDM (ABM).

quick_facts.label: Typical duration
quick_facts.value: A common full-time format is two years. IIM Ahmedabad describes its MBA-FABM as a two-year, full-time residential programme, and MANAGE describes its PGDM(ABM) as a two-year programme.

quick_facts.label: Relevant undergraduate foundation
quick_facts.value: B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture is a directly relevant feeder qualification. In its 2026 policy, MANAGE lists Agriculture among eligible agriculture-related disciplines; IIM Ahmedabad accepts agricultural sciences and agriculture-related degrees.

quick_facts.label: CAT relationship
quick_facts.value: CAT is commonly used for admissions but requirements are institute-specific. For the 2026–28 cycles, IIM Ahmedabad required a valid CAT 2025 score for domestic MBA-FABM applicants, and MANAGE required CAT 2025 for PGDM(ABM).

quick_facts.label: Credential availability
quick_facts.value: MBA (Agri Business Management) is listed among programmes at accredited agricultural universities in ICAR's December 2025 accreditation list, alongside related MBA/agribusiness variants.

sections.heading: Eligibility: agriculture graduates are well aligned, but rules differ by institute
paragraphs: A B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture graduate is strongly aligned with this pathway, but applicants should read each institute's current admission policy rather than assume a single national rule. For its 2026–28 PGDM(ABM), MANAGE required a bachelor’s degree in agricultural sciences or an agriculture-related discipline with at least 50% marks/equivalent CGPA (45% for SC/ST/PwD), and specified a four-year degree after Class XII. Its eligible list explicitly includes Agriculture, agricultural engineering, food technology, horticulture, fisheries, forestry and veterinary science, among other allied areas.

IIM Ahmedabad's 2026–28 MBA-FABM policy illustrates a broader alternative: it accepts agricultural science/agriculture-related bachelor’s or master’s degrees with 50% marks/equivalent CGPA (45% for SC/ST/PwD), and also permits graduates from non-agricultural disciplines who can demonstrate a strong interest in agriculture, food, rural and allied sectors through the required special form. Final-year students may be eligible provisionally, subject to the institute's documentation and completion conditions.
bullets: Check the required degree length: a four-year agriculture degree was required by MANAGE for its 2026 policy, whereas IIM Ahmedabad specified a minimum of three years after 10+2.
Check category-specific mark thresholds, recognised-university requirements, final-year conditions and any discipline/interest form before applying.
Treat B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture as a relevant academic parent, not as a guarantee of admission.

sections.heading: Duration and study format
paragraphs: The mainstream campus-based programme format is two years. IIM Ahmedabad describes MBA-FABM as a two-year, full-time residential MBA-level programme. MANAGE likewise states that its PGDM(ABM) is a two-year programme; its award is a postgraduate diploma rather than an MBA degree.

Because programme names and awards differ, applicants should verify whether an institution awards an MBA, a PGDM, or another postgraduate qualification, as well as whether residence, internships and campus attendance are compulsory.
bullets: MBA-FABM at IIM Ahmedabad: two-year, full-time residential programme.
PGDM(ABM) at MANAGE: two-year, AICTE-recognised PGDM programme.

sections.heading: Curriculum: management core plus food and agribusiness context
paragraphs: A specialised agri-business MBA combines general management preparation with sector-specific learning. IIM Ahmedabad states that its first year includes orientation, compulsory courses, a rural immersion module and summer placement; the second year includes sector-specific compulsory and elective courses. It describes the sector component as covering the socio-economic, political, policy and market environment for agribusiness, with decision-making shaped by uncertainty, perishability and seasonality.

The same programme reports three curriculum components: core management courses, compulsory food- and agribusiness-related courses, and electives. Its rural immersion module is mandatory and is intended to give students exposure to rural life, rural markets and links with socio-economic institutions. These are an example of curriculum design rather than a universal syllabus; course lists and credits vary by school.
bullets: Management foundation: functional and analytical management learning.
Sector focus: food, agriculture, agribusiness, rural and allied-sector markets and policy.
Applied components may include rural immersion, case work, group work, industrial exposure and a summer internship.

sections.heading: Admission: CAT-based shortlisting is common, followed by institute selection
paragraphs: CAT is a meaningful immediate parent for CAT-based MBA/PGDM agri-business routes, but candidates must apply separately to the relevant programme and meet its own policy. For IIM Ahmedabad’s 2026–28 MBA-FABM cycle, applicants needed a valid CAT 2025 score, an application to the programme and the stated eligibility conditions to be considered for Analytical Writing Test (AWT) and Personal Interview (PI). Its published process uses CAT and an application-rating score for shortlisting, then AWT, PI and a composite score for final selection.

For MANAGE’s 2026–28 PGDM(ABM), a valid CAT 2025 score was required and CAT percentile was identified as the primary shortlisting category; MANAGE also states that the IIMs have no role in MANAGE’s selection process or programme delivery. Dates, score cut-offs, interview procedures, reservation implementation and document deadlines change by admission cycle, so candidates should use the live admission notice for the intended batch.
bullets: Register for CAT in the relevant examination year where the target institute requires it.
Submit the institute-specific application; a CAT score alone does not create an application.
Prepare for subsequent evaluation such as written assessment, group discussion and/or interview where prescribed.
Verify the policy for the exact batch before relying on any cutoff or deadline.

sections.heading: Costs: compare batch-specific total charges, not just tuition
paragraphs: Fees are highly institution- and batch-dependent. As a dated example only, IIM Ahmedabad published a total programme fee of INR 27,50,000 for its 2025–27 PGP-FABM batch, including INR 20,10,000 tuition; it stated that the fee excluded personal costs such as boarding-related personal expenses, laptop, travel, clothing, stationery and laundry, and that future-batch fees would be announced later.

Applicants should obtain the current batch fee schedule directly from each institution and account for tuition, accommodation/mess where applicable, laptop, travel, insurance, deposits and other personal expenses. Do not treat a prior-batch fee as a quote for a future intake.
bullets: Dated fee reference: INR 27.50 lakh total for IIM Ahmedabad PGP-FABM 2025–27, not a forecast for later batches.
Ask for the current official fee schedule and refund/withdrawal rules before paying.
Compare scholarship, need-based aid and education-loan options only using the institution's current information.

sections.heading: Next options after graduation
paragraphs: Graduates can pursue management roles across agribusiness-linked industries and functions. IIM Ahmedabad identifies sectors including FMCG, agricultural inputs, agri-banking, agri-consulting, renewable energy, and food and agribusiness advisory; it identifies functions such as strategy, marketing, commodity trading, sales and distribution, supply-chain management, procurement, and research and advisory.

Entrepreneurship is another possible direction. IIM Ahmedabad states that it supports students interested in entrepreneurship through second-year electives and an in-house incubation centre. Actual job titles, hiring volumes, compensation and placement outcomes vary by institute, prior experience, location and market conditions; they should be checked in current official placement reports rather than inferred from programme branding.
bullets: Private-sector and cooperative value-chain roles: inputs, food/FMCG, procurement, trading, supply chain and distribution.
Financial and advisory roles: agri-banking, consulting, research and advisory.
Entrepreneurship: food, farm, rural-market or agriculture-service ventures, subject to individual opportunity and execution.
